commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an empty-array guard to the private browser Chrome profile decoration `setDeep` helper and removes the fallback empty-string key assignment.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence user-path reproduction is shown. The helper-level behavior is source-reproducible because current main would write `obj[""]` if called with an empty key array, but present production callers pass non-empty literal arrays.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of a defensive helper-only cleanup.

target.title: fix: guard setDeep against empty keys array in Chrome profile decoration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `setDeep()` with an empty `keys` array would access `keys[-1]` (which is `undefined`), and the `?? ""` fallback would silently create a property with an empty string key (`""`) on the target object.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add an early `if (keys.length === 0) return;` guard at the top of `setDeep`, and remove the now-unnecessary `?? ""` fallback on the final key access.

## User Impact

No user-visible change under normal operation. Prevents silent pollution of Chrome profile objects with empty-key properties in edge cases.

## Evidence

The `setDeep` function is called from `applyThemeColorOverrides` which iterates over known theme color keys, so empty `keys` would not occur in normal usage. The guard is defensive against future callers.
